keywords.serpWeakness: Top results are listicles from 2023 that haven't been updated; missing comparison of newest tools like Claude Pro and Gemini Advanced for writing.
userComplaints: Articles lack hands-on testing, just compile features from marketing sites.
No pricing breakdowns for teams or high-volume users.

keywords.keyword: AI writing assistant for blog posts
keywords.serpWeakness: Ranking pages are affiliate thin-content with no real blog-format examples or SEO integration advice.
userComplaints: Tools produce generic introductions and don't maintain tone across sections.
No built-in SEO scoring or keyword context.

keywords.serpWeakness: Top result is a 4-year-old roundup that omits newer low-cost options like Rytr, Copysmith, and free tiers of Writesonic.
userComplaints: Free tiers throttle output to the point of being unusable.
Hidden credit costs for templates like 'AIDA' or 'PAS'.

keywords.serpWeakness: Existing comparisons focus on feature lists, not real workflow speed or output quality tests.
userComplaints: Jasper's boss mode overpromises long-form; output drifts off topic after 500 words.
Copy.ai’s sentence rewriter inserts grammar errors in technical content.

keywords.serpWeakness: SERP dominated by tool homepages (SurferSEO, Frase) that lack independent comparison; no article tests raw output against Google's helpful content guidelines.
userComplaints: Generated SEO articles require heavy editing to avoid penalty.
Tools claim to bypass AI detection but fail originality checks.

keywords.serpWeakness: Only Sudowrite appears; no alternatives for long-form fiction with consistent character voice.
userComplaints: Sudowrite’s brainstorming produces clichéd plot twists.
No robust multi-chapter outlining with AI memory.

keywords.keyword: best free AI writing generator no sign-up
keywords.serpWeakness: Existing lists send users to tools that require email after first generation; no true-no-signup roundup.
userComplaints: Immediate email gating even on 'free' tools.
Ad-filled free versions ruin the experience.

keywords.keyword: AI copywriting tool with unlimited words
keywords.serpWeakness: Ranking pages are outdated (2022) and miss new entrants like Copymatic or TextCortex that offer generous caps.
userComplaints: Hidden fair-use limits throttle heavy users.
Sudden price hikes after 6 months.

keywords.keyword: how much does Jasper AI cost
keywords.serpWeakness: Jasper’s own pricing page ranks first; third-party breakdowns are thin on team plan details and real ROI examples.
userComplaints: Monthly word count runs out fast for bloggers.
Add-on fees for plagiarism checker and brand voices.

keywords.serpWeakness: General tool lists appear; nobody focuses on product descriptions with size/color variants or Amazon-optimized bullets.
userComplaints: Output lacks structured data (JSON-LD) for rich snippets.
No direct Shopify/WooCommerce integration.

keywords.keyword: best AI grammar checker vs writing assistant
keywords.serpWeakness: Grammarly dominates, but no article addresses when a full writing tool (like QuillBot) replaces grammar checkers entirely.
userComplaints: Grammarly’s premium suggestions flatten personal style.
QuillBot’s paraphrasing changes meaning unintentionally.

keywords.keyword: AI writing tool for non-native english speakers
keywords.serpWeakness: Very few dedicated resources; only general tools are mentioned, missing advanced grammar explanation and translation integration.
userComplaints: Tools correct errors but don’t explain the grammar rule.
No side-by-side translation view.

designConfig.voice: Modern, analytical — like a product reviewer who actually tests everything. Data-driven but conversational, with honest caveats.
designConfig.tagline: AI tools tested. No hype. Just what works.
